title: Person A: build runtime security and integration test matrix
description:
Build the Person A test suite around runtime enforcement. Tests should cover ALLOW, AUTO_APPROVE_EPHEMERAL_GRANT, ESCALATE_HUMAN, DENY, REPAIR, async pending, grant expiry, credential lease expiry, direct secret read denial, external Slack post denial, prompt-injection resistance, and redaction.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Test matrix passes locally; fixtures cover happy path, approval path, denial path, repair path, provider unavailable, user cancelled 1Password, and stale recipe/Qdrant commit; tests assert no secret-like values appear in outputs or persisted records.
notes: Sources: RFC-02, RFC-03, RFC-04, RFC-05 acceptance and invariants.

title: Person A: implement tools/call enforcement pipeline
description:
Implement the gateway runtime path for each proposed downstream tool call: resolve session, validate catalog and schema, normalize ToolIntent, build/update tool_call context snapshot, call async authorization check, create grant/lease when allowed, execute or block, redact output, and append audit event.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: linear.create_issue happy path executes with ALLOW/AUTO_APPROVE; slack.search_messages human-required path pauses until grant exists; slack.post_message external injection path is denied; REPAIR returns safe argument guidance; DENY and ESCALATE return structured safe errors.
notes: Sources: RFC-03 tools/call Pipeline, Downstream Execution, Error Model; RFC-00 Inline Enforcement.
close_reason: Implemented durable context snapshots/graph rows, dynamic MCP catalog auth tools, tools/call enforcement with auto grants and human requests, bounded grant consumption, and hash-chained runtime audit events in codex/complete-runtime-plan.

title: Person A: implement 1Password provider adapter
description:
Implement the real 1Password provider adapter behind the broker. Resolve op:// references only after a valid lease, inject into the selected execution boundary, scrub logs/output, and record provider operation metadata without storing secret values.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Adapter resolves a demo reference through the chosen 1Password flow; no decrypted value appears in agent-visible output, logs, Beads, Dolt, Qdrant, or UI fixtures; locked/cancelled/unavailable provider cases return ESCALATE_HUMAN or DENY according to policy; provider_request_id and credential_ref_hash are recorded.
notes: Adapter implementation added in platform/onepassword_provider.py and tests in platform/test_onepassword_provider.py. The adapter resolves op:// references only after an approved broker LeaseUseResult, calls op read --no-newline inside the broker boundary, wraps secret material in SecretValue with redacted str/repr, returns safe provider metadata with provider_operation_id and credential_ref_hash, denies invalid refs/unapproved leases, and escalates human setup/auth cases such as no account, locked vault, cancelled prompt, or unavailable CLI. Local live status on 2026-06-27: op is installed at /opt/homebrew/bin/op version 2.34.1, but op whoami reports no account found; onepassword-mcp is absent. Live demo-reference resolution remains blocked until a 1Password CLI account/session and non-secret demo reference are configured. No real secret was read or printed.

title: Person A: build MCP gateway preflight and tool catalog
description:
Build the ScopeMemory MCP gateway skeleton for auth.preflight_goal, auth.request_scope, auth.show_decision_proof, auth.explain_denial, tools/list, and session-scoped dynamic tool catalogs. This should expose a narrow catalog based on preflight, grants, and pending requests.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: auth.preflight_goal returns session_id, context snapshot/proof references, predicted tools/scopes from fixtures or Person B handoff, visible tools, auto-approved grants, and human-required requests; tools/list changes after grant approval/expiration; auth.show_decision_proof retrieves redacted proof data.
notes: Sources: RFC-03 auth.preflight_goal and tools/list; RFC-00 Runtime Loop.
close_reason: Implemented durable context snapshots/graph rows, dynamic MCP catalog auth tools, tools/call enforcement with auto grants and human requests, bounded grant consumption, and hash-chained runtime audit events in codex/complete-runtime-plan.

title: Person A: implement credential broker lease model
description:
Implement the credential broker core model without provider-specific secret resolution first. It should issue opaque CredentialLease objects only after policy allows, enforce session/tool/scope/resource/TTL/max-use binding, and expose non-secret evidence back to the gateway.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: CredentialLease shape matches RFC-04 and shared contracts; leases are opaque handles, not capabilities outside the authorized execution path; max uses and expiry are enforced; broker refuses to issue or use a lease when secret_exposed_to_agent could become true.
notes: Implemented in platform/credential_broker.py. CredentialLease now has broker-owned minting plus use authorization: session/tool/scope/resource binding checks, TTL expiry, max-use decrement/exhaustion, execution-boundary caller checks, and denial when a lease would expose secret material to the agent. Successful use returns non-secret evidence only: lease_id, credential_ref_id/hash, provider metadata, injection mode, remaining uses, and secret_exposed_to_agent=false. Covered by platform/test_credential_hooks.py.
close_reason: Implemented and tested Person A async authorization check contract and credential lease model

title: Person A: implement PolicyDecision precedence and proof traces
description:
Implement the deterministic decision reducer and proof trace builder around CozoDB outputs. The gateway needs a stable PolicyDecision object with decision, matched rules, facts used, context_path, dolt commit, qdrant index commit, credential lease reference, and proof hash.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Every policy result includes a proof trace matching the shared contract; rule/fact provenance is visible without exposing secrets; conflicting derived outcomes resolve by documented precedence; proof hash is stable for equivalent normalized decisions.
notes: Sources: RFC-02 Proof Trace; RFC-05 Audit Invariants; RFC-06 Person A contract.
close_reason: Implemented PolicyDecision/PolicyProofTrace production around Cozo outputs. Effective decisions use deterministic precedence, candidate decisions stay in the proof, facts/rules/context path are redacted and stable, proof_hash is deterministic, and platform/app.py plus platform/gateway/app.py store the structured proof.

title: Person A: implement async authz check lifecycle
description:
Implement the authorization check service path used by gateway and hooks. A check request compiles facts, invokes policy, records pending or terminal decision state, handles idempotent retries, and returns a proof_id or safe pending reason.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Repeated calls with the same idempotency key return the same check; PENDING checks cannot execute downstream tools; terminal ALLOW/AUTO_APPROVE/DENY/REPAIR/ESCALATE states are queryable; timeout and expiry behavior is tested; proof_id connects to the policy decision.
notes: Implemented in platform/authorization_checks.py. AuthorizationCheckService compiles/evaluates policy context, records facts_compiled and policy_evaluating transitions, maps terminal PolicyDecision outcomes into approved, auto_approved, denied, repairable, or waiting_for_human states, preserves idempotent retries by (session_id, idempotency_key), blocks execution unless approved/auto_approved, exposes proof_id from PolicyProofTrace.proof_hash, supports cancellation and expiry, and is covered by platform/test_authorization_checks.py.
close_reason: Implemented and tested async authorization check lifecycle

title: Person A: build typed fact compiler for policy
description:
Implement the ScopeMemory fact compiler that converts Session, ToolIntent, schema validation, resource hints, current grants, recipe hits, context paths, and credential binding metadata into deterministic policy facts. This is the boundary between memory context and CozoDB policy.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Compiler emits the fact classes from RFC-02 for session, tool, resource, recipe, graph, grant, credential, and judge facts; invalid or missing facts are represented explicitly; fixture inputs produce stable fact sets with hashes; no fact contains decrypted credentials.
notes: Sources: docs/engineering-plan/plan/RFC-02-authorization-policy-proofs.md Fact Classes; RFC-01 Gateway Query Patterns.
close_reason: Implemented compile_policy_facts in platform/cozo_policy.py. It converts graph/gateway context into typed Cozo fact rows for session, tool, resource, recipe, graph/context path, grants, credentials, judge facts, policy flags, and violations; produces stable fact strings and fact_set_hash; tests verify no secret values are projected.

title: Person A: implement CozoDB policy rule bundle
description:
Implement the Datalog-backed policy engine in CozoDB over compiled facts. Encode hard denies, repairable schema failures, current-grant allows, auto-approved ephemeral grants, human escalation, credential constraints, and graph/context-path requirements.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: CozoDB rules derive exactly one effective decision for each test case; hard deny outranks allow and auto-approval; repair is only used for safe structural fixes; policy rejects unreified Qdrant hits, missing context paths, direct secret reads, external Slack post exfiltration, and admin scopes without break-glass.
notes: Sources: RFC-02 Decision Rules, Hard Denies, Auto-Approval Requirements.
close_reason: Implemented COZO_POLICY_RULES in platform/cozo_policy.py with embedded pycozo Client execution, parameterized fact relations, hard-deny rules, repair, current grant allow, auto-approved ephemeral grants, human escalation, credential safety, context-path enforcement, unreified recipe-hit denial, and deterministic fallback. Verified with embedded Cozo temp install.

title: Person A: verify local 1Password provider readiness
description:
Verify the local 1Password prerequisites before building the broker: op CLI availability, authentication behavior, demo vault/item/reference shape, and whether the chosen flow is CLI, service account, op run, or environment injection. Do not store any secret value in the repo, Beads, logs, Dolt, Qdrant, or screenshots.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: op version and auth mode are documented in the bead notes; a non-secret demo credential reference shape is chosen; failure modes for locked vault, cancelled prompt, and unavailable provider are recorded; no secret material is printed or persisted.
notes: Updated 2026-06-27 after op CLI install. Current local 1Password readiness: desktop app present at /Applications/1Password.app; op CLI present at /opt/homebrew/bin/op version 2.34.1; op account/session not configured (op whoami reports no account found); onepassword-mcp still absent at /Applications/1Password.app/Contents/MacOS/onepassword-mcp; OP_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN not set during readiness check. No secret values were read, printed, stored, or persisted. Non-secret demo shape chosen: credential_ref_id=credref_linear_sales, provider=onepassword, credential_class=linear.oauth_token, injection_mode=gateway_header; setup-only placeholder reference shape op://ScopeMemory Demo/Linear API Token/token remains unverified. Failure modes recorded in docs/engineering-plan/research/credential-injection-hooks-readiness.md.
close_reason: Verified local 1Password provider readiness and documented fail-closed setup-required result

title: Person A: specify async authorization check contract
description:
Turn the implied async authorization check into an explicit runtime contract. Define check_id, idempotency_key, request facts, PENDING behavior, terminal decisions, polling/subscription behavior, timeout, expiry, cancellation, and proof linkage. This closes the gap between tools/call and slower human/provider paths.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Contract covers created, facts_compiled, policy_evaluating, waiting_for_human, approved, auto_approved, denied, repairable, expired, and cancelled states; terminal decision precedence is documented; execution remains blocked until a terminal positive decision; examples exist for immediate ALLOW, PENDING human approval, DENY, and REPAIR.
notes: Implemented in platform/policy_contracts.py and platform/authorization_checks.py. AuthorizationCheck contract covers created, facts_compiled, policy_evaluating, pending, waiting_for_human, approved, auto_approved, denied, repairable, expired, and cancelled. AuthorizationCheckService enforces idempotency by (session_id, idempotency_key), maps PolicyDecision to terminal/non-terminal states, blocks execution except approved/auto_approved, supports cancellation and expiry, and exposes proof_id linkage. Examples/tests cover immediate ALLOW, human escalation, DENY, REPAIR, idempotency, and expiry in platform/test_authorization_checks.py.
close_reason: Implemented and tested Person A async authorization check contract and credential lease model

title: Person A: verify RFC-07 runtime baseline
description:
Run and inspect the existing RFC-07 SQLite ReBAC demo before promoting it into Phase 2 runtime. Capture how demo inputs map to Session, ToolIntent, PolicyDecision, context_path, grants, and denial reasons. This is Person A's baseline sanity check before changing runtime semantics.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: python demo/run_demo.py all exits 0 or the bead is updated with concrete failures; Person A has notes mapping RFC-07 demo scenes to Phase 2 runtime contracts; ALLOW, DENY, and ESCALATE_HUMAN examples are identified for later policy tests.
notes: Baseline verified on 2026-06-27 with python3 demo/run_demo.py all. Demo scenes map to Phase 2 decisions: ALLOW linear.create_issue with grant_linear_001 and context path sess_demo_001 -> recipe_sales_renewal_v3 -> linear.create_issue -> linear:issues:create -> linear_team:SALES; DENY slack.post_message to external-partners because recipe does not predict tool and write/external is unsafe; ESCALATE_HUMAN slack.search_messages because predicted slack:channels:history scope is human_required without grant.
close_reason: Baseline verified with python3 demo/run_demo.py all; notes map ALLOW, DENY, and ESCALATE_HUMAN scenes to Phase 2 runtime facts.

title: Person B: build memory demo and product surface
description:
Own the governed memory loop and visible ScopeMemory demo. Implement Dolt schema and seed data; Workflow Authorization Recipe model; Qdrant recipe indexing and retrieval; mocked Slack data and prompt-injection scenario; web UI for live session, access requests, proof tree, timeline, recipe review, credential lease inspector, and index refresh status; learning worker for recipe diffs; demo script and fixtures; fixture catalog for access requests, grants, leases, policy decisions, proof traces, audit events, accepted recipes, proposed recipe diffs, and denial paths; and the integration harness that can swap fixtures for the real gateway.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: SessionGoal -> RecipeHits -> PredictedScopes -> AccessRequests -> UI State works against fixtures; Sales Renewal Prep happy path, approval path, denial path, and recipe-learning path are demoable; UI never displays secrets; Dolt recipe diff and Qdrant provenance are visible; fixture-backed demo can be replaced incrementally by Person A gateway outputs.

title: Person A: build runtime enforcement path
description:
Own the security-critical execution path for the ScopeMemory MVP. Implement the MCP gateway path for auth.preflight_goal, tools/list, and tools/call; the CozoDB policy engine and fact compiler; ALLOW, AUTO_APPROVE, ESCALATE, DENY, and REPAIR decisions; 1Password credential broker lease shape; pre-tool-use hook adapter; mock Linear and Slack execution wrappers; and proof trace generation.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: ToolIntent -> PolicyDecision -> optional CredentialLease -> ExecutionResult -> AuditEvent works through mocked Linear and Slack calls; policy decisions include proof traces; credentials are represented as opaque leases; pre-tool-use hook simulation blocks direct secret reads; external Slack post injection path is denied.

title: Define shared ScopeMemory JSON contracts
description:
Define the integration contracts for the two-person MVP split before substantial implementation starts. Cover Session, ToolIntent, PolicyDecision, AccessRequest, Grant, CredentialLease, RecipeHit, and AuditEvent, plus stable fixture IDs for the Sales Renewal Prep demo story.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Versioned JSON schema or typed contract artifacts exist for all shared contract names; happy-path, approval-path, denial-path, credential-lease, proof-trace, and recipe-hit fixtures validate against them; RFC-06 contract list remains aligned with the artifacts.
close_reason: Implemented typed shared runtime contracts in platform/policy_contracts.py for Session, ToolIntent, PolicyDecision, AccessRequest, Grant, CredentialLease, RecipeHit, and AuditEvent; policy tests exercise the contract-backed fixture path.

title: Person A: publish runtime handoff fixtures for Person B
description:
Produce stable runtime outputs that Person B can consume: preflight response, policy decisions, proof traces, access request, grant, credential lease metadata, audit events, denied external Slack post, repaired bad arguments, and redacted execution results.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Fixture outputs conform to shared JSON contracts; Person B can replace static UI fixtures with Person A gateway outputs incrementally; no fixture contains secrets; each fixture names the scenario and source test that generated it.
notes: Sources: RFC-06 Integration Boundary and Person B fixture catalog; RFC-03/RFC-04 runtime outputs.

title: Person A: implement pre-tool-use hook adapter and exec wrapper
description:
Implement the hook adapter for agent hosts that can intercept tool calls. Normalize hook input, block direct secret reads, route eligible tool calls through ScopeMemory, and rewrite shell commands to scopememory exec --lease only after policy and broker checks.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Hook simulation denies op read and secret-writing commands; safe eligible CLI calls are rewritten to ScopeMemory execution wrappers; hook output never includes decrypted credentials, plaintext .env, Authorization headers, or sensitive op:// paths; hook-unavailable fallback is gateway-only tools.
notes: Sources: RFC-04 PreToolUse Hook Adapter and Hook Enforcement Examples; RFC-03 Shell Tools.

title: Person A: implement mock Linear and Slack execution wrappers
description:
Implement downstream execution wrappers for mocked Linear and Slack behind the gateway. These are not the visible product surface; they are controlled runtime adapters that receive credentials only from the broker/gateway boundary and return redacted, structured results.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Mock Linear create/search/comment and Slack search/post wrappers exist; wrappers reject calls without a valid policy decision; outputs pass redaction; mocked Slack prompt-injection content is treated as untrusted data and cannot expand session goal or grant access.
notes: Sources: RFC-03 Downstream Execution, Result Redaction, Prompt Injection Defense; RFC-06 Person A mock execution wrappers.

title: Person A: implement runtime audit and event hashing
description:
Implement the runtime audit writer for Person A-owned events: preflight requested, tool call requested, policy decision, access request created, grant issued, credential lease issued/used, downstream call executed, output redacted, denial returned.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: Events are hash-chained; every policy decision points to a Dolt commit or fixture commit placeholder; credential events record secret_exposed_to_agent=false; audit records never contain raw secret values, bearer tokens, private keys, or full sensitive references.
notes: Sources: RFC-05 Audit Log and Audit Invariants; RFC-01 policy_decisions and session_events.
close_reason: Implemented durable context snapshots/graph rows, dynamic MCP catalog auth tools, tools/call enforcement with auto grants and human requests, bounded grant consumption, and hash-chained runtime audit events in codex/complete-runtime-plan.

title: Person A: implement runtime grants and access request handoff
description:
Implement Person A's runtime side of AccessRequest and Grant creation. The UI belongs to Person B, but the gateway must create safe request records, consume approvals, issue short-lived grants, enforce TTL/call counts, and resume blocked checks.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: ESCALATE_HUMAN creates or updates an access request with safe summary and proof_id; approved requests mint bounded grants; revoked/expired grants block future calls; grant issuer, reason, TTL, resource constraint, and proof reference are recorded.
notes: Sources: RFC-01 Access Request and Grant; RFC-03 Session Lifecycle; RFC-06 integration steps.
close_reason: Implemented durable context snapshots/graph rows, dynamic MCP catalog auth tools, tools/call enforcement with auto grants and human requests, bounded grant consumption, and hash-chained runtime audit events in codex/complete-runtime-plan.

title: Record two-person implementation split
description:
Update the ScopeMemory engineering plan with the agreed Person A/Person B implementation split, shared JSON contract boundary, and durable follow-up work for implementation.

## Required Skills
beads
acceptance_criteria: RFC-06 records runtime enforcement ownership, memory/demo/product ownership, shared JSON contracts, and implementation follow-up issues are created for the split.
close_reason: Recorded two-person implementation split in RFC-06 and created follow-up workstream issues

title: Migrate Beads workspace to server mode
description: Track the migration from the embedded Dolt data directory to Beads server mode. Acceptance: Dolt-native backup exists, bd dolt status reports server mode, bd commands work after restart, and issue data is preserved.
close_reason: Migrated Beads workspace to server mode with local backup and verified server health

title: Record ScopeMemory owner planning decisions
description:
Update the ScopeMemory engineering plan with owner decisions: v1 zero-knowledge means zero secret exposure to agents and persistence; MVP uses real 1Password; Slack is mocked; policy engine is CozoDB; local SR planning is accepted with no live pooled arbiter pass required.

## Required Skills
beads,use-sr-code
acceptance_criteria: Plan docs, decisions, and SR Code mirror reflect the owner decisions; no implementation starts.

title: Write ScopeMemory engineering plan/spec
description:
Create a planning-only SR Code-style engineering plan/spec for ScopeMemory based on INITIAL_ROUGH_PLAN.md. Cover the whole memory-informed authorization project, including MCP gateway, Dolt/Qdrant/policy/audit/LLM judge flows, demo/MVP sequencing, and the new zero-knowledge credential injection design using password managers such as 1Password through pre-tool-use hooks. Do not implement product code.

## Required Skills
use-sr-code,beads
acceptance_criteria: Plan/spec artifacts exist in the ScopeMemory repo; SR Code planning package exists; source-grounded research and judge notes are recorded; implementation is not started; open owner decisions are explicit.
